title: Which Antigravity surface should I use? 2.0 vs CLI vs IDE vs SDK
summary: Google Antigravity is an agent-first software development platform for building, orchestrating, and monitoring AI agents. It provides multiple development surfaces including a desktop app (2.0), CLI, IDE, and SDK that enable agents to perform development tasks autonomously.
writeup: This video compares the four surfaces of Google Antigravity, an agent-first software development platform: the 2.0 desktop app, the CLI, the IDE, and the SDK. It covers how each interface fits different agentic workflows for building, orchestrating, and monitoring AI agents that integrate with code editors, terminals, and browsers. The takeaway is matching a surface to the task: the desktop app, command line, editor, or programmatic SDK access.
